data.definition: Software as a Service - online platform hosted by a server for users to access. (Microsoft office, Netflix)
Large Language Model — an AI trained on huge amounts of text that predicts the next word to generate human-like responses.
Wipes conversation history, keeps file edits. Use when context is getting large.
Smarter version of /clear; compresses history instead of deleting it, preserves key info. Better default than /clear for mid-session use.
Can restore context even after a /clear if you regret it.
Claude thinks through the approach before touching any files. Critical for large tasks to avoid compounding mistakes.
Shows exactly what changed before you commit, so nothing surprises you.
Sets a target Claude works toward across many turns. Good for "finish the backend" type sessions.
Ask a side question without polluting the main task context. Useful when you're curious about something mid-build.
Picks up a previous session.
Forks the conversation into a separate session for experimenting without messing up your main thread.
Shows how many tokens you've used and what it's costing.
Switch models mid-session (e.g. drop to Sonnet for boilerplate, use Opus for hard problems).
Adjusts reasoning depth. Lower effort = faster/cheaper.
Checks for config issues.
Lists everything available in your current version.
Run once in a new project; generates a CLAUDE.md file that keeps Claude oriented every time you start a new session.
Opens settings to change theme, default model, and other Claude Code options.
Edit your CLAUDE.md memory files directly, so you can tweak the instructions Claude reads each session.
Create and manage custom subagents — specialized helpers Claude can hand specific tasks to.
Reviews a GitHub pull request and reports issues it finds.
Runs a security review of the pending changes on your current branch.
Toggles Fast mode — same Opus model, faster output. Handy when you want quick responses.
